Bitfield Description: Source for virtual stop (VIRTUAL_STOP_L and VIRTUAL_STOP_R)
Bitfield Decode:
0x0: Virtual stop relates to ramp-generator position XACTUAL.
0x1: Virtual stop relates to encoder position X_ENC.

Bitfield Description: Automatic motor stop during active right virtual stop condition
Enables automatic motor stop during active left virtual stop condition

The soft stop mode always uses the deceleration ramp settings DMAX, V1, D1, V2, D2, VSTOP, and TZEROWAIT for stopping the motor. A stop occurs when the velocity sign matches the reference switch position (REFL and VIRTUAL_STOP_L for negative velocities; REFR and VIRTUAL_STOP_R for positive velocities) and the respective switch stop function is enabled. A hard stop also uses TZEROWAIT before the motor becomes released. Note: Do not use soft stop in combination with StallGuard2/4. Use soft stop for StealthChop2 operation at high velocity. In this case, hard stop must be avoided as it could result in severe overcurrent.

Enable stop by StallGuard2/4. Disable to release motor after stop event. Program TCOOLTHRS for velocity threshold. Do not enable during motor spin-up. Wait until the motor velocity exceeds a certain value, where StallGuard2/4 delivers a stable result. This velocity threshold should be programmed using TCOOLTHRS.
Latch encoder position to ENC_LATCH upon reference switch event
Activates latching of the position to XLATCH upon an inactive edge on the right reference switch input REFR. The active level is defined by pol_stop_r.
Activates latching of the position to XLATCH upon an active edge on the right reference switch input REFR. Activate latch_r_active to detect any spurious stop event by reading status_latch_r.
Activates latching of the position to XLATCH upon an inactive edge on the left reference switch input REFL. The active level is defined by pol_stop_l.
Activates latching of the position to XLATCH upon an active edge on the left reference switch input REFL. Activate latch_l_active to detect any spurious stop event by reading status_latch_l.
Swap the left and the right reference switch input REFL and REFR
Bitfield Decode:
0x0: Default assignments 
0x1: REFL and REFR switched

0x0: Not inverted, active high: a high level on 
REFR stops the motor 
0x1: Inverted, active low: a low level on REFR 
stops the motor

Bitfield Description: Sets the active polarity of the left reference switch input
Bitfield Decode:
0x0: Non-inverted, high active: a high level on 
REFL stops the motor 
0x1: Inverted, low active: a low level on REFL 
stops the motor

Bitfield Description: Enables automatic motor stop during active right reference switch input. The motor restarts when the stop switch is released.
Enables automatic motor stop during active left reference switch input. The motor restarts when the stop switch is released
Enable tolerance once the motion controller reached the target position.
Bitfield Decode:
0x0: The P-controller ignores absolute errors below 
the tolerance. 
0x1: The P-controller ignores absolute errors below tolerance only when the pos_reached flag (M1_RAMP_STAT[9]) of the motion controller is set to 1.

Bitfield Description:
Proportional parameter for position P-regulator. P=0 disables P-regulator. P > 0 enables P-regulator function.
Unit 10.0, no fractional part.
